title: eriogonum fastigiatum
intro: Eriogonum fastigiatum, commonly known as Pineleaf Buckwheat, is a perennial shrub that belongs to the Eriogonum genus, native to the western United States. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Eriogonum fastigiatum include its ability to soothe skin irritations, reduce fever, and aid in wound healing. Eriogonum fastigiatum can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and its leaves and stems can be used fresh or dried for herbal remedies.
